metadata.purpose: Step-by-step emergency response guidance for Indian crisis scenarios

medical_review.reviewed_by: Emergency Medicine Specialists, Indian Red Cross

metadata.legal_disclaimer: These protocols are for emergency guidance only and do not replace professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of qualified health providers with any questions regarding a medical condition. In life-threatening emergencies, call 112 immediately.

protocols.title: Clothes Catch Fire (Stop, Drop, Roll)
protocols.situation: Your clothing has caught on fire.
protocols.content: If your clothes catch fire, the flames can spread rapidly to your face and airway. The Stop-Drop-Roll technique smothers flames without fanning them.

steps.action: Do not panic or run—running fans the flames.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: STOP immediately where you are.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: DROP to the ground and lie flat.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover your face with your hands to protect it.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: ROLL over and over until the fire is completely out.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not run.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not wave your arms or try to beat the flames with hands.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not remain standing.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

source.org: Indian Red Cross Society / St John Ambulance Association (India)

immediate_action.text: Do not panic or run—running fans the flames.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

time_critical.golden_period: Fire can double in size every 60 seconds

protocols.title: Trapped in a Smoke-Filled Room
protocols.situation: You are in a room filling with smoke and your primary exit is blocked.
protocols.content: Smoke rises and accumulates near the ceiling. Staying low keeps you in cleaner air and improves visibility.

steps.action: Stay low to the ground where air is cleanest.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Crawl on hands and knees towards the nearest exit or window.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover your mouth and nose with a damp cloth to filter smoke.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If possible, seal door cracks with wet towels or clothes to slow smoke entry.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Signal for help from a window if you cannot escape.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not stand upright in thick smoke.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not open windows if fire is directly outside—this can draw fire in.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not hide in closets or under beds.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

source.org: Directorate General Fire Services, Civil Defence & Home Guards (MHA)

immediate_action.text: Stay low to the ground where air is cleanest immediately.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: Evacuating a High-Rise Building During Fire
protocols.situation: A fire alarm has sounded in your high-rise building and you need to evacuate.
protocols.content: High-rise fires require orderly evacuation using stairs. Elevators may fail or open onto fire floors.

steps.action: Before opening any door, feel it with the back of your hand.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the door is hot, do not open it—find an alternate route.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the door is cool, open it slowly and proceed to the nearest fire exit staircase.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: DO NOT use elevators (lifts).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Close all doors behind you as you leave to slow fire spread.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move quickly but calmly down the stairs, holding the handrail.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Once outside, move to the designated assembly point away from the building.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use elevators under any circumstances.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not go back to collect belongings.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not prop open fire doors.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

source.document_title: National Building Code of India 2016, Part 4: Fire and Life Safety
source.page_or_section: Page 81 (Annex D: Guidelines for Fire Drill and Evacuation Procedures for High Rise Buildings)

immediate_action.text: Opening any door immediately.

protocols.title: Using a Portable Fire Extinguisher (PASS Method)
protocols.situation: You have found a small, contained fire and have access to a fire extinguisher.
protocols.content: Portable extinguishers are effective only on small fires. Use the PASS technique and always keep your back to an exit.

steps.action: Ensure you have a clear escape route behind you.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: PULL the pin at the top of the extinguisher.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: AIM the nozzle at the base of the fire, not the flames.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: SQUEEZE the lever slowly and evenly.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: SWEEP the nozzle from side-to-side, covering the base of the fire until it is out.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not aim at the flames—aim at the base.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not fight a fire if it is spreading rapidly or blocking your exit.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use water extinguisher on electrical or oil fires.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

source.org: Directorate General Fire Services, Civil Defence & Home Guards (MHA)

immediate_action.text: Use fire extinguisher if safe or evacuate immediately.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: First Aid for Minor Burns (Superficial Burns)
protocols.situation: A person has a small, red, painful burn with no blisters after a minor fire incident.
protocols.content: Minor burns affect only the outer skin layer. Prompt cooling reduces pain and tissue damage.

steps.action: Cool the burn immediately under cool (not cold) running water for at least 10-15 minutes.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Gently remove rings, watches, or tight clothing from the area before swelling starts.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover the burn with a sterile, non-stick dressing or clean cloth.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Give the person paracetamol for pain if needed.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not apply butter, toothpaste, oil, or any home remedies.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not apply ice directly to the burn.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not break any blisters that may form.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Cool the burn immediately under cool (not cold) running water for at least 10-15 minutes.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: Responding to an Electrical Fire
protocols.situation: An electrical appliance is sparking, smoking, or has caught fire.
protocols.content: Electrical fires pose shock hazards. Water conducts electricity and must never be used.

steps.action: If safe, immediately switch off the main power supply to the area.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the fire is small, use a Carbon Dioxide (CO2) or Dry Chemical Powder (DCP) extinguisher.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: NEVER use water on an electrical fire.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the fire is spreading, evacuate immediately and call Fire Services.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Never throw water on an electrical fire—risk of electrocution.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not touch any exposed wiring or appliance.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to unplug burning appliances.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: First Person to Discover a Fire
protocols.situation: You are the first person to discover a fire in a building.
protocols.content: Early warning saves lives. Alerting others and calling emergency services must happen immediately.

steps.action: Activate the nearest fire alarm pull station if available.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Shout 'FIRE!' loudly to alert people nearby.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call the Fire Services (101 or 112) immediately—give correct address and location of fire.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Evacuate the building using the nearest safe exit.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not assume someone else has called for help.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not assume someone else has raised the alarm.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to fight the fire unless it is very small and you are trained.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay your own evacuation.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Escape Route Blocked by Fire or Smoke
protocols.situation: The hallway or staircase is completely blocked by heavy smoke or flames.
protocols.content: If you cannot evacuate safely, shelter in place, seal the room, and signal rescuers.

steps.action: Return to your apartment or room and close the door.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use wet cloths, towels, or tape to seal cracks around the door and any vents.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call Fire Services (101/112) and tell them your EXACT location (apartment/floor/room number).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Go to a window, open it slightly for fresh air if safe, and signal to firefighters with a bright cloth or flashlight.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Stay low near the floor where air is cleaner.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to run through heavy smoke or flames.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not go to the roof unless specifically instructed.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not jump from windows unless absolutely necessary and only if rescue is below.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: First Aid for Severe Burns (Deep Burns)
protocols.situation: A person has large, deep burns which may be white, charred, or numb.
protocols.content: Severe burns are life-threatening emergencies requiring immediate professional medical care.

steps.action: Call for an ambulance immediately (102/108 or 112).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not try to remove any clothing that is stuck to the burn.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not immerse large severe burns in cold water—this can cause hypothermia.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover the burn area with a clean, dry, non-stick cloth or sterile dressing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Reassure the casualty and monitor their breathing and consciousness until help arrives.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not peel off stuck clothing or material.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not apply any substances (cream, oil, ice) to severe burns.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give food or water if the person may need surgery.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Kitchen Grease/Oil Fire in a Pan
protocols.situation: A pan of cooking oil on the stove has caught fire.
protocols.content: Oil fires are extremely dangerous. Water causes burning oil to splash and spread the fire explosively.

steps.action: Turn off the heat source immediately if it is safe to do so.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover the pan with a metal lid or heavy, damp cloth to smother the flames.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Slide the lid over the pan carefully to avoid splashing burning oil.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Leave the pan covered until it cools completely.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the fire is unmanageable, evacuate and call Fire Services.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: NEVER use water to put out a grease/oil fire.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: NEVER attempt to carry the burning pan outside.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use flour—it can explode.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

source.org: Directorate General Fire Services, Civil Defence & Home Guards (MHA)

immediate_action.text: Turn off the heat source immediately if it is safe to do so.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: First Aid for Smoke Inhalation
protocols.situation: A person rescued from a fire is coughing, having difficulty breathing, or is confused.
protocols.content: Smoke inhalation damages airways and can cause delayed complications. All smoke inhalation victims need medical evaluation.

steps.action: Move the person to fresh air immediately, away from the smoke source.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Help them into a comfortable position—usually sitting up to ease breathing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Loosen any tight clothing around their neck and chest.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call for an ambulance, as smoke inhalation can cause serious internal damage.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Stay with the person and monitor their condition until help arrives.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not allow the person to return to the smoky area.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not ignore symptoms even if they seem mild—they can worsen.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give food or water if the person is having breathing difficulty.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Move the person to fresh air immediately.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: Trapped in a Car During Forest Fire
protocols.situation: You are driving and become surrounded by a forest fire.
protocols.content: A vehicle provides better protection than being exposed outside. Stay inside and call for help.

steps.action: Do not drive through heavy smoke—visibility is near zero.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Pull over to a clearing or area with least vegetation if possible.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Close all windows and vents completely.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn on your headlights and hazard lights so rescuers can see you.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Get down on the floor of the car and cover yourself with a wool blanket or jacket.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Stay in the vehicle—it provides better protection than being outside.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use your mobile phone to call for help and give your location.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not drive blindly through heavy smoke.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not get out and run into the forest.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not park near dense vegetation.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Forest Fire Approaching: Shelter in Place
protocols.situation: A forest fire is approaching your home and you are advised to shelter in place.
protocols.content: If evacuation is not possible, create a defensible space inside your home.

steps.action: Close all windows, doors, and vents to prevent embers from entering.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move flammable furniture, curtains, and other items away from windows.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Fill sinks, bathtubs, and buckets with water.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Stay inside and away from outside walls and windows.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep your emergency supply kit with you.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Monitor news/radio for updates.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not leave doors or windows open.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not stay near windows where radiant heat can injure you.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not wait until the last moment to prepare.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

immediate_action.text: Close all windows immediately.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: Fire in a School: Orderly Evacuation
protocols.situation: A fire alarm sounds or fire is discovered in a school.
protocols.content: School evacuations require discipline. Teachers must lead students out safely and account for everyone.

steps.action: Teachers should direct students to form a single line.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Evacuate the classroom in an orderly manner.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Close the classroom door behind you.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Follow the designated evacuation route to the pre-determined assembly point.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Take a head count at the assembly point to ensure all students are accounted for.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: No one should re-enter the building until cleared by Fire Services.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not allow students to run back for bags or belongings.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use elevators.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not allow anyone to re-enter the building.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: First Aid for Electrical Shock with Fire
protocols.situation: A person received an electric shock from a faulty wire that has also started a small fire.
protocols.content: Electrical shock can cause cardiac arrest and burns. The scene must be made safe before touching the victim.

steps.action: DO NOT touch the person if they are still in contact with the electrical source.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Switch off the main power supply immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If you cannot turn off the power, use a dry, non-conductive object (wooden stick, dry rope) to separate the person from the source.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Once safe, check for breathing. If not breathing, start CPR if trained.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Treat any burns according to burn first aid protocol.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call for an ambulance immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not touch the casualty before power is disconnected.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use water near live electricity.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use metal objects to separate the person from the source.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

source.org: Indian Red Cross Society / St John Ambulance Association (India)

immediate_action.text: DO NOT touch the person if they are still in contact with the electrical source.

protocols.title: Suspected LPG Leak (No Fire Yet)
protocols.situation: You smell gas and suspect your LPG cylinder is leaking, but there is no fire yet.
protocols.content: LPG vapour is heavier than air and collects at floor level. Any spark can cause explosion.

steps.action: Do not panic. Do not switch any electrical switches on or off.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Extinguish all open flames, lamps, and incense sticks immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Open all doors and windows to ventilate the area.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn the regulator knob to the 'OFF' position.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the leak persists, carefully move the cylinder to an open, well-ventilated space.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call your gas distributor's emergency number from a safe distance outside.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not switch any electrical appliances on or off.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not light matches, lighters, or any flame.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use mobile phones inside the gas-filled area.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

immediate_action.text: Do not panic.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

time_critical.golden_period: Fire can double in size every 60 seconds

protocols.title: LPG Cylinder Nozzle/Pipe Catches Fire
protocols.situation: The nozzle or pipe of your LPG cylinder is on fire.
protocols.content: A burning cylinder will not explode as long as gas is escaping. Smother the flame and shut off the gas.

steps.action: Do not panic—a cylinder on fire will not explode if gas is flowing out.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use a thick, wet cloth (jute bag, bedsheet) and wrap it around the small fire at the nozzle to smother it.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn the regulator knob to the 'OFF' position.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If you cannot extinguish the fire, evacuate everyone and call Fire Services (101/112).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not approach if flames are large or spreading.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay evacuation if you cannot control the fire.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not throw water directly on the cylinder.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Creating a Home Fire Escape Plan (Pre-Emergency)
protocols.situation: Planning ahead to ensure your family can escape a fire safely.
protocols.content: A practiced escape plan can mean the difference between life and death in a fire.

steps.action: Draw a simple floor plan of your home, marking all doors and windows.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Identify at least two escape routes from every room.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Choose an outside meeting place (specific tree, neighbor's house) where everyone will gather.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Practice your home fire drill at least twice a year with everyone.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Teach children how to escape on their own in case adults are not nearby.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rely on only one exit route.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not skip practice drills—muscle memory saves lives.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not forget to include infants, elderly, and pets in your plan.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

primary.when_to_call: When fire is detected or smoke visible

immediate_action.text: Draw a simple floor plan of your home immediately.

immediate_triggers: If fire is spreading or smoke increases

protocols.title: Assisting a Person with Disability to Evacuate
protocols.situation: You need to help a person with mobility issues evacuate during a fire.
protocols.content: People with disabilities may need extra assistance. Know the location of refuge areas in buildings.

steps.action: Alert the person to the fire and ask how you can best assist them.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the person uses a wheelchair and elevators cannot be used, check for a designated 'Area of Refuge' near the staircase.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

protocols.content: Vehicle fires can spread rapidly. Get everyone out and away from the vehicle immediately.

steps.action: Warn oncoming traffic if possible.

protocols.title: First Aid for Chemical Burns
protocols.situation: A person's skin has been splashed with a corrosive chemical during a fire or chemical leak.
protocols.content: Chemical burns require immediate and prolonged flushing with water to remove the chemical.

steps.action: Immediately flush the affected skin with large amounts of cool, running water for at least 20 minutes.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Carefully remove contaminated clothing or jewelry while flushing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If it is a dry chemical (like powder), brush it off the skin BEFORE flushing with water.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Loosely cover the burn with a dry, sterile dressing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Seek immediate medical help.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not try to neutralize the chemical with another substance.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay irrigation.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rub the affected area.

steps.action: Keep a bucket of water or sand nearby at all times.

steps.action: Wear thick cotton clothing—avoid synthetic fabrics that melt.

steps.action: Never try to re-light a firework that has not gone off.

steps.action: Place diyas (lamps) and candles on flat, non-flammable surfaces away from curtains or paper.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Supervise children at all times.

what_to_avoid.action: Do not re-light malfunctioning fireworks.

what_to_avoid.action: Do not allow children to use fireworks unsupervised.

protocols.title: Arriving at a Road Accident Scene – Making the Area Safe
protocols.situation: You reach a road accident scene and see injured people on the road.
protocols.content: Scene safety is the first priority. An unsafe rescuer becomes a second victim.

steps.action: Before going near, look for dangers: oncoming traffic, unstable vehicles, fire, smoke, electrical wires.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If safe, apply the handbrake of involved vehicles to prevent movement.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Place stones/bricks against tyres to stop vehicles rolling.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep bystanders away from the road; create space around injured persons.

steps.action: Do not move injured people unless they are in immediate danger.

protocols.content: Movement should only happen when the danger of staying is greater than the danger of moving.

steps.action: First try to make the area safe without moving the casualty (handbrake, block tyres, warn traffic).

steps.action: Keep the patient warm and call 112.

protocols.title: Accident Victim Unresponsive and Not Breathing (CPR)
protocols.situation: Road accident victim collapsed, unresponsive, not breathing or only gasping.
protocols.content: Without blood circulation, brain damage begins within minutes. Compression-only CPR by bystanders saves lives.

steps.action: Ensure scene safety (traffic, fuel, glass).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Shake shoulders and shout to check responsiveness.

steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Push hard and fast in centre of chest (100-120 per minute).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Continue without long pauses until ambulance arrives or victim starts breathing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay CPR to wait for ambulance.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not check pulse for more than 10 seconds.

what_to_avoid.action: Do not place severed part directly on ice.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.situation: You see spilled fuel, smoke, or smell petrol/diesel around crashed vehicles.

steps.action: Do not smoke or allow any open flames near the scene.

steps.action: Keep yourself and others upwind and away from visible fuel.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move bystanders further back; create clear access for emergency vehicles.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move injured victims only if staying there is more dangerous.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 and clearly mention possible fuel leak / fire risk.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not smoke or use open flames.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Good Samaritan Rights When Helping Accident Victims
protocols.situation: You want to help at a road accident but are afraid of legal trouble.
protocols.content: Indian law protects Good Samaritans who help road accident victims. You cannot be harassed by police or hospitals.

steps.action: Know that as a Good Samaritan, you have legal protection for helping.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: You are not liable for any civil or criminal action for helping.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: You may leave the hospital immediately after informing staff.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: You cannot be forced to reveal personal details or pay for treatment.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Police cannot force you to be a witness; it is your choice.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Help without fear — the law is on your side.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not hesitate to help due to fear of legal problems.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not assume you will be detained by police.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Transporting an Injured Person to Hospital (When Ambulance Is Not Available)
protocols.situation: In a remote area, ambulance will not arrive soon and the victim needs hospital urgently.
protocols.content: Improper transport can worsen injuries. Use safe methods even when improvising.

steps.action: Only transport if ambulance will not arrive in time and person's condition is worsening.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If spinal injury is suspected, keep head, neck, and body in a straight line during transfer.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use a flat rigid surface (door, plank) as a makeshift stretcher if possible.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Secure the person so they do not slide during transport.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep the person warm.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Continue to monitor breathing during transport.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Drive carefully — sudden braking or turns can worsen injuries.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep the person calm and still.

steps.action: Monitor breathing continuously.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 and request urgent transport to a hospital with anti-venom (ASV).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give anti-nausea medicine without a doctor.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give water or food.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Position the person on their side (recovery position) to prevent choking on vomit.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Dark urine or unable to pass urine
protocols.situation: A person bitten by a snake passes very dark (black/brown) urine or cannot pass urine.
protocols.content: This indicates muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis) or red blood cell destruction, leading to acute kidney injury.

steps.action: Recognize this as a severe complication indicating kidney damage.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep the person lying down and immobilized.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Note the time when urine output decreased or changed color.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not give food or water.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Rush to the hospital immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Inform doctors specifically about the dark urine.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not ignore changes in urine.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay hospital care.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Recognize this as a severe complication indicating kidney damage.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Refusing traditional healer interventions
protocols.situation: A traditional healer wants to treat the snake bite with herbal paste and incisions before going to the hospital.
protocols.content: Traditional remedies delay life-saving Anti-Snake Venom (ASV) and often cause severe infections or bleeding.

steps.action: Politely but firmly refuse traditional treatments.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not allow cutting, burning, or application of substances to the wound.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not allow tight tourniquets.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Explain that only Anti-Snake Venom (ASV) can neutralize the poison.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Go directly to the nearest hospital immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not waste time at a traditional healer.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not allow incisions on the bite.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Training Manual / Emergency Module for CHO
source.page_or_section: Note of Caution: harmful traditional practices

immediate_action.text: Politely but firmly refuse traditional treatments.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any suspected poisonous snake bite

protocols.title: Deciding which hospital to go to
protocols.situation: You need to decide which hospital to take a snake bite victim to.
protocols.content: Not all clinics stock Anti-Snake Venom (ASV). Government hospitals are mandated to keep it.

steps.action: Go to the nearest government hospital, District Hospital, or Primary Health Centre (PHC).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: These government facilities stock free Anti-Snake Venom (ASV).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 or the hospital ahead of time if possible to confirm ASV availability.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the nearest facility lacks ASV, they will provide first aid and arrange safe referral.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not waste time going to small private clinics that may not stock ASV.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not go to clinics without ASV stock.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay transport searching for private care when government care is nearer.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Protocol for Initial Management of Snakebite

immediate_action.text: Go to the nearest government hospital immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any suspected poisonous snake bite

protocols.title: No visible fang mark after suspected bite
protocols.situation: Someone feels they were bitten by a snake in the grass, but there is no visible fang mark.
protocols.content: Krait bites often leave marks so small they are invisible. All suspected bites require medical observation.

steps.action: Take the incident seriously even without visible marks.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep the person calm and immobilized.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Remove tight items from the suspected limb.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Transport to a hospital for observation.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: The hospital will observe for at least 24 hours to ensure no delayed symptoms appear.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not assume no mark means no venom.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not skip hospital observation.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Take the incident seriously even without visible marks.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any suspected poisonous snake bite

protocols.title: Prolonged transport to hospital (2 hours+)
protocols.situation: You are transporting a snake bite victim to a hospital, and the journey will take hours.
protocols.content: During long transports, proper positioning and constant monitoring are crucial to prevent deterioration.

steps.action: Keep the victim lying down during the entire transport.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Ensure the bitten limb remains immobilized and at heart level.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Monitor breathing and level of consciousness continuously.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the person vomits, turn them on their side.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not give food, water, or any medication during transport.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Note any new symptoms that develop to report to the hospital.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give food or water during the journey.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not remove the splint or bandage.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Keep the victim lying down during the entire transport.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any suspected poisonous snake bite

protocols.title: Evacuation Ordered / Flood Warning Issued
protocols.situation: Flood warning issued by authorities; evacuation ordered.
protocols.content: Prepare to leave immediately. Keep your emergency kit ready and secure your home before routes are cut off.

steps.action: Listen to the radio or TV for official updates from authorities.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Pack an emergency kit with documents, valuables, essential medicines, and a flashlight.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn off the main electrical switch and gas valve in your house.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move livestock and pets to higher ground.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Evacuate immediately to the designated safe shelter.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not panic or run.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not ignore evacuation orders hoping the water will recede.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Listen to the radio or TV for official updates from authorities.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Flash Flood Imminent
protocols.situation: Flash flood imminent (water rising rapidly with no time to pack).
protocols.content: Flash floods move with deadly speed. Survival depends on getting to high ground instantly.

steps.action: Do not wait for official instructions to move.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Abandon your belongings—your life is more important.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move immediately to higher ground or the highest floor/roof of a sturdy building.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Stay out of dry creek beds, drains, and valleys.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not try to collect valuables.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to secure the house if water is already rushing in.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Do not wait for official instructions to move immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Walking Through Flooded Urban Areas
protocols.situation: Needing to walk through a flooded street or urban area.
protocols.content: Urban floodwaters hide deadly hazards like open manholes, sharp debris, and strong undercurrents.

steps.action: Avoid walking through flowing water.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If you must walk, walk where the water is standing and not moving.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Always use a pole or stick to check the firmness of the ground in front of you.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use the stick to detect open manholes, ditches, or sunken areas.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Wash yourself with soap and clean water immediately after contact with floodwater.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not walk in moving water—even 15 cm (6 inches) can sweep you off your feet.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not let floodwater touch open wounds.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Avoid walking through flowing water immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Trapped Inside a House as Water Rises
protocols.situation: Trapped inside a house as floodwaters rise.
protocols.content: Move upwards safely and signal for help. Avoid enclosed spaces where water can trap you.

steps.action: Move to the upper floors or the roof of the building.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Take your emergency kit, mobile phone, and a flashlight with you.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Signal for help using a bright cloth, mirror, or flashlight.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Wait for NDRF or local rescue boats.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT climb into a closed attic, as you may become trapped by rising water.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to swim to safety if the current is strong.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Move to the upper floors or the roof of the building.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Driving a Vehicle into Flooded Roads
protocols.situation: Driving a vehicle and encountering a flooded road.
protocols.content: Vehicles float easily and can be swept away in shallow moving water.

steps.action: Turn around and find an alternate route.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If your vehicle stalls in water, abandon it immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Leave the vehicle and move to higher ground on foot if safe to do so.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT drive through flooded areas or standing water.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not stay inside a trapped car—it can be swept away by just 60 cm (2 feet) of moving water.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Do's and Don'ts for Common Disasters

immediate_action.text: Turn around and find an alternate route immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Water Enters House Near Electrical Outlets
protocols.situation: Water enters the house and threatens electrical outlets.
protocols.content: Electrocution is a leading cause of death during urban floods. Cut power immediately.

steps.action: Stay away from power lines and electrical wires.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn off the main power switch from the distribution board immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If you see sparks or hear buzzing, leave the area immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not touch any electrical equipment or switches if you are wet or standing in water.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to disconnect appliances while standing in floodwater.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Stay away from power lines and electrical wires immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Rescuing Someone Swept Away (Reach or Throw)
protocols.situation: Someone is swept away in the flood current or drowning.
protocols.content: Do not become a second victim. Use objects to reach the person instead of jumping in.

steps.action: Use the 'Reach or Throw' method.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Throw a rope, a lifebuoy, or any floating object (empty sealed cans/bottles).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Extend a long stick, branch, or pole for them to grab.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 or alert NDRF/local authorities immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT jump into the floodwater to save them unless you are a trained water rescuer.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not let the victim pull you into the water.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Use the 'Reach or Throw' method immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Unconscious Victim Pulled from Water (CPR)
protocols.situation: Victim pulled from floodwater is unconscious and not breathing.
protocols.content: Start CPR immediately. Do not waste time trying to drain water from the lungs.

steps.action: Lay the person flat on their back on a safe, dry surface.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Check the airway and remove any visible mud or debris from the mouth.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Start CPR immediately: 30 chest compressions followed by 2 rescue breaths.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Continue until the person breathes or professional help arrives.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT waste time trying to pump or drain water from the lungs.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not delay starting compressions.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Lay the person flat on their back on a safe, dry surface.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Hypothermia After Water Rescue
protocols.situation: Victim rescued from floodwater is awake but shivering violently, pale, and confused.
protocols.content: Prolonged exposure to floodwater causes severe body heat loss.

steps.action: Move the person to a dry, wind-shielded shelter.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Remove all wet clothing immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Dry the person and wrap them in dry blankets or clothes, covering the head.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the person is fully conscious and can swallow safely, give them warm, sweet non-alcoholic drinks.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Seek medical help.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT give alcohol.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT rub their skin vigorously.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give fluids if the person is confused or unable to swallow.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Move the person to a dry immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Safe Drinking Water During Floods
protocols.situation: Thirsty and needing water while trapped by floods.
protocols.content: Floodwater is highly toxic. Only consume water that has been purified or sealed.

steps.action: Drink only bottled water or water stored in sealed containers before the flood.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If clean water is unavailable, boil available water vigorously for at least 1 minute.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If boiling is not possible, use halogen/chlorine water purification tablets as directed.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep all food covered and safe from floodwater contact.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT drink floodwater under any circumstances.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use floodwater to wash dishes or brush teeth.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: National Disaster Management Guidelines: Management of Floods

immediate_action.text: Drink only bottled water or water stored in sealed containers before the flood.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Encountering Snakes in Floodwater
protocols.situation: Wading through floodwater and encountering a snake.
protocols.content: Snakes lose their habitats during floods and seek refuge in trees, homes, and debris.

steps.action: Stop moving and stay calm.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Slowly back away from the snake to give it space to escape.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Always use a stick to tap the ground and bushes ahead of you when walking.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If bitten, keep the limb immobilized and seek medical help immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt to hit, corner, or catch the snake.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not reach into hidden places or debris piles with bare hands.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Stop moving and stay calm immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Improvised Flotation Devices
protocols.situation: Trapped in deep floodwater with no life jacket.
protocols.content: Use household items to stay afloat while waiting for professional rescue.

steps.action: Do not panic; conserve your energy.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Grab hold of empty sealed water bottles, plastic jerry cans, or inflated vehicle tire tubes.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: In rural areas, thick branches or banana tree trunks can be used as makeshift rafts.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Hold firmly on to the flotation device and wait for NDRF rescue boats.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not try to swim long distances in floodwater.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not tie the flotation device to your body, as it may snag on underwater debris.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Do not panic immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Caught in a Fast-Moving Current
protocols.situation: Caught in a fast-moving flood current.
protocols.content: Defensive swimming helps protect your head and vital organs from underwater impacts.

steps.action: Roll onto your back immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep your feet pointed downstream (in the direction the water is flowing).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep your head up to see where you are going.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use your feet to push off rocks, debris, or obstacles to protect your head.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Steer yourself diagonally towards the nearest safe bank or sturdy structure and grab hold.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not exhaust yourself by fighting or swimming directly against the strong current.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not try to stand up in fast-moving water deeper than your knees.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Roll onto your back immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Wounds and Cuts from Floodwater
protocols.situation: Sustaining a cut or wound while moving through dirty floodwaters.
protocols.content: Floodwater contains bacteria that cause severe infections like Leptospirosis and Tetanus.

steps.action: Get out of the floodwater as soon as possible.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Wash the wound thoroughly with clean (bottled or boiled) water and soap.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Apply an antiseptic cream or liquid.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover the wound with a waterproof bandage or clean dressing to prevent floodwater from entering.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Seek medical attention for a tetanus shot and antibiotics if advised.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not let the wound remain exposed to floodwater or mud.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not ignore redness or swelling around the cut.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Community First Aid Response (CFAR) Pocket Guide

immediate_action.text: Get out of the floodwater as soon as possible immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Returning Home After Flood
protocols.situation: Returning home after floodwaters have receded.
protocols.content: Post-flood homes harbor structural dangers, toxic mud, snakes, and electrical hazards.

steps.action: Wear closed sturdy shoes and carry a stick to check for snakes or debris hidden in the mud.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Open all doors and windows to ventilate the house and let it dry.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Clean and disinfect all surfaces.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Throw away any food, medicine, or cosmetics touched by floodwaters.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do NOT enter the house if there is visible structural damage or it looks unsafe.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not turn on any electrical appliances or the main switch until checked by an electrician.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Wear closed sturdy shoes and carry a stick to check for snakes or debris hidden in the mud.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Family Readiness in Flood-Prone Areas
protocols.situation: You live in a flood-prone area and need to prepare before water enters.
protocols.content: Early preparation allows for safe, orderly evacuation when warnings are issued.

steps.action: Ensure all family members know the safe routes to the nearest shelter or raised pucca house.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Tune to local radio/TV for warnings and advice.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep an emergency kit ready.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep dry food, drinking water, and clothes ready.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not wait for the water to reach your doorstep before making a plan.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rely solely on one escape route.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Do's and Don'ts for Common Disasters

immediate_action.text: Ensure all family members know the safe routes to the nearest shelter or raised pucca house.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Protecting Important Documents and Valuables
protocols.situation: Evacuating soon: you must protect documents, medicines, and valuables from water damage.
protocols.content: Crucial documents and medicines are irreplaceable and necessary for post-flood recovery.

steps.action: Gather warm clothing, essential daily medications, valuables, and personal papers.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Place them in secure waterproof bags or plastic folders.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep this pack with your emergency kit so it can be grabbed instantly during evacuation.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not leave original documents in low-lying drawers or cupboards.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not pack heavy unnecessary items that slow down evacuation.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Do's and Don'ts for Common Disasters

immediate_action.text: Gather warm clothing immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Reducing Damage Inside the House
protocols.situation: Floodwater may enter your home soon: you need to reduce damage inside the house.
protocols.content: Moving items above the expected water line minimizes property loss and contamination.

steps.action: Raise furniture, clothing, and appliances onto beds, tables, or higher surfaces.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move high-value items to the highest possible safe place in the house (loft or upper floor).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep emergency items ready near the exit so they can be carried quickly if evacuation is ordered.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not leave toxic chemicals (like pesticides or cleaners) on the floor where they can spill into floodwater.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not risk your life trying to save heavy furniture.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Do's and Don'ts for Common Disasters

immediate_action.text: Raise furniture immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Nutrition for Vulnerable Groups During Flood
protocols.situation: Flood conditions at home with children/pregnant woman: risk of weakness/dehydration.
protocols.content: Vulnerable groups deteriorate rapidly without clean food and water during disasters.

steps.action: Keep safe, dry food ready that has not been exposed to floodwater.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Ensure children and pregnant women do not remain on an empty stomach.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Provide simple, light meals.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Ensure they drink only boiled or safely bottled water.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not feed them any food that has touched floodwater.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not let them consume untreated tap water.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

source.document_title: Do's and Don'ts for Common Disasters

immediate_action.text: Keep safe immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Handling Rumours and Panic
protocols.situation: Conflicting messages or rumours about dam breaches or massive floods.
protocols.content: Panic and false information cause unnecessary accidents and disorganized evacuations.

steps.action: Do not panic.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Listen only to official radio, television, or government social media handles for information.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Follow warnings and suggestions given by local authorities (District Magistrate/SDMA).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Help calm neighbors and share only verified information.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not listen to or spread unverified rumours.

protocols.situation: Finding someone fainted or unresponsive in a room with a gas smell.
protocols.content: Gas can displace oxygen (asphyxiation) or poison the victim directly.

steps.action: Assess your own safety; do not enter if you feel dizzy.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Hold your breath or use a wet cloth over your face.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Drag the victim out to fresh air immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 for an ambulance.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Start CPR if the victim is not breathing once in fresh air.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Loosen tight clothing around the neck.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not enter without notifying others first.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not attempt CPR inside the gas-filled area.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Assess your own safety immediately.

protocols.title: Gas Contact with Eyes
protocols.situation: Eyes burning or vision blurred after exposure to leaked gas.
protocols.content: Immediate flushing prevents permanent chemical damage to the cornea.

steps.action: Move to fresh air immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Flush eyes with large amounts of clean, running water.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Maintain flushing for at least 15-20 minutes.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep eyelids wide open during flushing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Remove contact lenses immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not rub the eyes.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rub your eyes.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use eyewashes or eye drops without medical advice.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Chemical Splash on Skin
protocols.situation: Liquid from a gas leak or chemical splashes on clothing/skin.
protocols.content: Liquefied gases can cause severe frostbite or chemical burns.

steps.action: Remove all contaminated clothing and jewelry immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Flush the affected skin with water for at least 20 minutes.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If the chemical is a dry powder, brush it off BEFORE washing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover the area loosely with a clean cloth.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Seek medical help for burns.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not apply ointments or home remedies (oil/butter).
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rub the affected skin.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Hissing Sound from LPG Cylinder
protocols.situation: Hearing a loud hissing sound from the regulator or cylinder pipe.
protocols.content: Hissing indicates a high-pressure, active leak. Explosion risk is imminent.

steps.action: Evacuate everyone from the house immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If you can do it safely in one second, turn off the regulator.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not attempt to fix or plug the leak.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Open windows only if safe and near the exit.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call the distributor or 1906 from outside.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not try to plug the leak with soap or tape.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not go near the sound with any light source.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Evacuate everyone from the house.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Gas Leak in Basement or Enclosed Space
protocols.situation: Suspecting a gas leak in an underground or low-lying area.
protocols.content: Gases like LPG and H2S settle in basements, displacing air and causing suffocation.

steps.action: Do not enter the basement if you suspect a leak.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Alert anyone inside to evacuate immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn off the main external gas supply valve.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not operate any electrical equipment like pumps or lights.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call the fire service (101).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not enter to check the source of the smell.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not turn on ventilation fans inside the basement.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Do not enter the basement if you suspect a leak.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Ammonia Leak (Refrigeration/Industrial)
protocols.situation: Exposure to a pungent, sharp smell like strong cleaning fluid.
protocols.content: Ammonia is highly corrosive to the respiratory tract and eyes.

steps.action: Evacuate cross-wind to a safe area.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Use a wet cloth to cover your face.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If exposed, flush eyes and skin with water immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Sit upright to ease breathing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Drink only if fully conscious.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not stay in low-lying areas.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rub your eyes.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Evacuate cross-wind to a safe area immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Chlorine Gas Leak (Water Plant/Pool)
protocols.situation: Seeing a yellow-green gas cloud or smelling bleach-like sharp odor.
protocols.content: Chlorine is heavier than air and highly toxic when inhaled.

steps.action: Evacuate immediately to higher ground.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move upwind or cross-wind from the source.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Cover face with a wet handkerchief.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Remove contaminated clothing once in fresh air.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Rush to hospital if coughing or chest pain develops.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not move downwind.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use water directly on the source of the chlorine leak.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Evacuate immediately to higher ground.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) Sewer Gas Exposure
protocols.situation: Exposure to 'rotten egg' smell near sewers or manholes.
protocols.content: H2S is deadly and causes 'olfactory fatigue' (you stop smelling it as it gets more toxic).

steps.action: Move to fresh air immediately upwind.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not enter a manhole or pit to rescue someone who fainted.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call for a professional rescue team with SCBA (breathing apparatus).
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 for medical help.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not enter the pit to rescue others without breathing gear.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not assume it's safe because you can no longer smell it.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Move to fresh air immediately upwind.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

steps.action: Do not stop to clean it up.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Open windows only if you can do it without inhaling more fumes.

steps.action: Move to fresh air.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Seek medical help if coughing or chest tightness occurs.

what_to_avoid.action: Do not stay to 'air out' the room if fumes are strong.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not mix different cleaning liquids.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_triggers: Immediately if situation worsens or help is delayed

protocols.title: Suspicious Smell in Public Place (Chemical Terrorism)
protocols.situation: Detecting a strange chemical odor or seeing fainted people in a mall/subway.
protocols.content: In case of deliberate chemical release, rapid evacuation and covering airways is vital.

steps.action: Cover your mouth and nose with a damp cloth immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Evacuate the area quickly and calmly.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Move upwind once outside.

steps.action: Follow instructions from security or NDRF responders.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not stop to collect belongings.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not rush the exits to avoid stampede.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Cover your mouth and nose with a damp cloth immediately.

immediate_triggers: Immediately for any critical condition

protocols.title: Gas Leak During Earthquake
protocols.situation: Detecting gas smell immediately after an earthquake tremor.
protocols.content: Secondary fires from gas leaks are a major cause of death after earthquakes.

steps.action: Complete 'Drop, Cover, Hold On' first.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Once shaking stops, check for gas smell immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn off the main gas valve/cylinder.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Evacuate the building—the structure may be weak.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Alert authorities from outside.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not light matches or use torches.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use any electrical switches.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Leaking Regulator or Pipe During Change
protocols.situation: Detecting gas leakage while changing or connecting a new cylinder.
protocols.content: Faulty washers or loose connections are common sources of leakage.

steps.action: Stop the connection process immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Check the valve rubber 'O' ring for damage.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If leaking from regulator, remove it and put the safety cap on the cylinder.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Ventilate the room.

steps.action: Call the distributor for a mechanic.
