protocols.title: Gas Leak While Sleeping
protocols.situation: Waking up to a strong gas smell at night.
protocols.content: Gas inhalation while sleeping can be fatal before waking up.

steps.action: Do not turn on any lights or fans.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Waken all other family members and evacuate immediately.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Open windows only if you are already next to them.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn off the main cylinder valve if safe.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call for help from outside.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not use your phone as a flashlight.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not go back inside for anything.

protocols.title: Safe Post-Leak Re-entry
protocols.situation: Returning home after a leak has been controlled.
protocols.content: Pockets of gas can remain in cabinets or low areas after the main leak is stopped.

steps.action: Wait for clearance from the fire department or gas company.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Keep all windows open for at least 30 minutes before staying inside.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Verify there is no lingering smell.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Clean all surfaces exposed to gas residue.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not enter until declared safe.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not turn on the stove immediately.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

immediate_action.text: Wait for clearance from the fire department or gas company.

protocols.title: Visible Mist/Vapor Cloud from Pipeline
protocols.situation: Seeing a shimmering mist or white fog near a gas pipeline.

steps.action: Shout to warn others to leave the area.

protocols.situation: A house fire has started and there is a gas cylinder in the room.

steps.action: If the cylinder is already hot or reached by flames, evacuate immediately.

protocols.title: Expired Safety Hose Check
protocols.situation: Inspecting your LPG safety hose (Suraksha tube).
protocols.content: Expired or cracked hoses are the leading cause of domestic leaks.

protocols.situation: You are experiencing domestic violence and need immediate help or rescue.
protocols.content: In immediate danger, prioritize safety and contact emergency services. Legal protection is available under the Domestic Violence Act.

steps.action: Reach out to a trusted neighbor, friend, or family member.

steps.action: If possible, leave the house and go to a safe place.

protocols.title: Trapped in Building During Riot or Curfew
protocols.situation: You are trapped in a building during a violent communal riot or curfew.
protocols.content: Stay indoors and follow police instructions to avoid being targeted.

steps.action: Stay indoors and do not go near windows or doors.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Turn off all lights.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Do not make noise or shout from windows.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 and give your location.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Follow official police announcements.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not leave until police announce it is safe.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not go onto rooftops or balconies.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: First Aid for Strangulation or Choking Injury
protocols.situation: A person has been strangled or choked and is having difficulty breathing.
protocols.content: Strangulation can cause delayed airway swelling and requires urgent medical evaluation.

steps.action: Remove any physical obstruction from around the neck.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 for an ambulance.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Help the person sit upright to ease breathing.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Loosen tight clothing around the neck and chest.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: If breathing stops, start CPR if trained.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not leave the person alone.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

what_to_avoid.action: Do not give food or drink.
what_to_avoid.reason: This can cause harm or worsen the situation

protocols.title: Online Threat of Violence
protocols.situation: You receive a threat of violence on social media or online.
protocols.content: Preserve evidence and report immediately to protect yourself legally and physically.

steps.action: Take screenshots and preserve all evidence.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Block the person on the platform.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Report the threat to the platform.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: File a complaint on cybercrime.gov.in or at the nearest Cyber Crime Police Station.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

steps.action: Call 112 if the threat is imminent and credible.
steps.reason: This step is critical for safety

what_to_avoid.action: Do not engage with the harasser.
